evals.prompt: Add a rule to the existing RegisterAuthor command that the name must be at least 2 characters.
evals.expected_output: A rule added to the RegisterAuthor CommandValidator<RegisterAuthor> (FluentValidation), not thrown from Handle(). No new command or handler created.
assertions: Rule is added in CommandValidator<T> (FluentValidation), not inside Handle()
Does not throw for business rejection (returns a validation result)
Does not create a new command or handler class

evals.prompt: Add a rule that an author cannot be registered if one with the same name already exists, enforced correctly under concurrency.
evals.expected_output: Either an event-store IConstraint for the uniqueness guarantee, or a state-dependent rule that injects the read model into Handle() and returns Result<AuthorRegistered, ValidationResult> with a typed error (DCB pattern). Never a thrown exception.
assertions: Concurrency-safe rule uses an IConstraint, or the read model in Handle() returning Result<TEvent, ValidationResult>
Business rejection is a typed ValidationResult, never a thrown exception
Fetched/computed data is provided via Provide() where appropriate
